---
title: Serialize
description: Replace the default JSON serializer with a custom one.
icon: ArrowLeftRight
---

Replaces the default JSON serializer with a custom implementation. Use this to support types that `JSON.stringify` cannot handle: `Date`, `Map`, `Set`, `BigInt`, `undefined`, and so on.

## Setup

```ts
import { serializePlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/serialize";
import { superjsonSerializer } from "@justwant/cache/serializers/superjson";
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { redisAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/redis";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis }),
  plugins: [serializePlugin({ serializer: superjsonSerializer() })],
});
```

## Options

| Option | Type | Required | Description |
|--------|------|----------|-------------|
| `serializer` | `CacheSerializer` | Yes | Object with `serialize` and `deserialize` methods |

## `CacheSerializer` interface

```ts
interface CacheSerializer {
  serialize: (value: unknown) => string;
  deserialize: (raw: string) => unknown;
}
```

## Custom serializer

```ts
import { serializePlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/serialize";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  plugins: [
    serializePlugin({
      serializer: {
        serialize: (v) => JSON.stringify(v),
        deserialize: (raw) => JSON.parse(raw),
      },
    }),
  ],
});
```
